originalTitle: Wall St ends lower as chip weakness offsets solid earnings, data
summary: STORY: Wall Street's main indexes ended lower on Thursday, with the Dow dropping two-tenths of a percent, the S&P 500 shedding about half a percent and the tech-heavy Nasdaq losing almost one-and-a-half percent. Chip stocks tumbled despite bellwether TSMC posting a 77% jump in quarterly profit, it's shares falling more than 2%. Mel Casey, senior portfolio manager at FBB Capital Partners, said the pullback demonstrated the lofty expectations for a sector that has soared by nearly 70% so far this year. "You just have so many investors in the same trade here and really banking on every little component of the value chain, like, humming along at maximum pace. And so anything that's a little bit shy of, say, the whisper, or anything that's as good as expected but not better than expected is seen almost as a disappointment. And so that's what you're seeing today. But there's nothing you could really point to in the Taiwan Semi report to feel bearish about." Memory-chip makers were among the biggest laggards, with SanDisk, Western Digital, Seagate Technology and Intel down between about five-and-a-half and twelve-and-a-half percent. :: Netflix Elsewhere in the market, shares of Netflix tumbled more than 7% in extended trading after the streaming giant's third-quarter revenue and earnings projections hovered below Wall Street targets. The company also said it would reduce the amount of information it discloses about consumers' viewing hours as it seeks new avenues of growth. Shares of UnitedHealth Group closed higher after the company beat Wall Street earnings estimates and hiked its 2026 forecast. But shares of United Airlines fell as surging oil prices weighed on its forward guidance. Meanwhile, generally upbeat economic data on Thursday showed solid core retail sales, a drop in jobless claims and surging manufacturing activity in the Northeast.

chineseTitle: 由于芯片疲软抵消了稳健的盈利和数据，华尔街收低
chineseSummary: 故事：华尔街主要股指周四收低，其中道指下跌十分之二，标准普尔 500 指数下跌约半个百分点，以科技股为主的纳斯达克指数下跌近百分之半。尽管领头羊台积电公布季度利润增长 77%，但芯片股仍暴跌，其股价跌幅超过 2%。 FBB Capital Partners 高级投资组合经理梅尔·凯西 (Mel Casey) 表示，此次回调表明了人们对今年迄今已飙升近 70% 的行业的崇高期望。 “这里有如此多的投资者从事同一行业，并且真正依赖价值链的每一个小组成部分，比如以最快的速度前进。因此，任何有点害羞的事情，比如说，耳语，或者任何与预期一样好但不比预期更好的事情，都几乎被视为令人失望。这就是你今天看到的。但在台湾半导体的报告中，没有什么可以真正让你感到悲观的。”内存芯片制造商是最大的落后者之一，SanDisk、西部数据、希捷科技和英特尔的股价下跌了约百分之五到百分之十二。 :: Netflix 在市场其他地方，Netflix 的股价在盘后交易中下跌了 7% 以上，此前这家流媒体巨头的第三季度收入和盈利预测徘徊在华尔街目标之下。该公司还表示，在寻求新的增长途径时，将减少披露的有关消费者观看时间的信息量。联合健康集团 (UnitedHealth Group) 的盈利超出华尔街预期并上调 2026 年预测，该公司股价收高。但由于油价飙升对其前瞻性指引造成压力，联合航空股价下跌。与此同时，周四总体乐观的经济数据显示核心零售销售强劲，有所下降

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
